summary: This cluster investigates the composition, distribution, and community structure of benthic marine algae along the intertidal zone of the East Coast of Korea, specifically at Chumunjin. The research identified a total of 76 algal species, comprising 10 Chlorophyta, 22 Phaeophyta, and 44 Rhodophyta, with key species including *Sargassum thunbergii* and *Corallina pilulifera*. Significant seasonal biomass variation was observed, with maximum biomass occurring in summer and minimum in autumn, while *Sargassum thunbergii* exhibited seasonal cover ranging from 20 to 23% and *Corallina pilulifera* at 13%. The studies employed UPGMA clustering and detrended correspondence analysis to delineate distinct vertical distribution zones of green and brown algae.
